name: PR Review Pack
description: A starter pack for coding agents reviewing pull requests, enforcing quality gates, and identifying regressions.

codex.notes: Skills, GitHub PR inspection metadata, and portable managed review prompts/templates/tools validate for Codex and local coding-agent workflows.

claude-code.notes: Agent Skills, Claude review plugin wrapper, and portable managed review capabilities are supported for Claude Code users.

useCases: Review pull requests for correctness, regressions, maintainability, and missing tests before merge.
Summarize GitHub PR risk for human reviewers with clear findings, severity, and merge readiness.
Check CI failures, flaky tests, and quality gate gaps that should block or delay a change.
Help local coding agents prepare focused review notes without drifting into unrelated refactors.
examplePrompts: Review this PR for correctness, regression risk, test gaps, and maintainability issues. Lead with blocking findings.
Summarize the merge readiness of this pull request, including CI status, risky files, missing tests, and owner follow-ups.
Inspect this diff as a senior reviewer and identify the smallest set of changes needed before approval.
Turn these review comments into actionable fixes, preserving the existing scope and avoiding unrelated cleanup.

capabilities.name: PR review pack guidance
capabilities.content: Use the PR Review Pack to help local coding agents review pull requests with reviewer discipline. Lead with correctness, regression risk, test coverage, maintainability, security/privacy exposure, CI health, and merge readiness. Keep feedback scoped to the diff unless a broader issue directly affects the change.

capabilities.name: Senior PR review
capabilities.content: Review this PR as a senior engineer. Prioritize blocking correctness bugs, regressions, missing tests, security/privacy risks, data migration risk, and maintainability issues. Provide findings first with severity, file or area, why it matters, and the smallest acceptable fix. End with merge readiness.
